title: Black Box Data After a Semi Crash in Dallas, Texas
twitter: 🚛 Semi crash in Dallas? The truck's black box can be OVERWRITTEN in days. ECM/EDR data could prove your case — but you have to act NOW. Call 855-WRECKMATCH (855-897-3256) or visit https://www.wreckmatch.com/blog/black-box-data-after-a-semi-crash-in-dallas-texas
linkedin:
If you or someone you know has been involved in a semi-truck collision on I-35E, I-30, or another Dallas-area highway, one critical piece of evidence is often overlooked — the truck's black box.

Commercial trucks are equipped with Electronic Control Modules (ECM) and Event Data Recorders (EDR) that capture speed, braking, engine load, hours of service, and more. This data can be the difference between a dismissed claim and a successful recovery. The problem? It can be overwritten within days if no preservation demand is issued.

Texas spoliation rules exist to protect victims, but only if the right legal steps are taken immediately. That's why connecting with an experienced Dallas truck accident attorney — fast — is essential.

At WreckMatch, we've built a network of 800+ vetted law firms across the country, including top-tier Texas truck accident attorneys who know exactly how to preserve black box data and build a compelling case.

If you're a truck accident victim in the Dallas area, don't wait. The evidence won't.

📋 Get matched with a qualified attorney today: https://www.wreckmatch.com/#form

reddit_title: Semi truck crash in Dallas — does the truck's black box data actually help injury claims, and how long before it's gone?
reddit_body:
If you've been in a collision with an 18-wheeler in the Dallas area, one of the most important things to understand is that commercial trucks carry black box technology — specifically an ECM (Electronic Control Module) and sometimes an EDR (Event Data Recorder). These devices log speed, braking patterns, throttle input, hours of service, and more. In a liability dispute, this data can be incredibly powerful evidence.

The catch is that this data doesn't last forever. Depending on the truck and its systems, ECM/EDR data can be overwritten in as little as 30 days — sometimes sooner. To protect it, an attorney needs to send a spoliation letter demanding the trucking company preserve all data immediately. Texas courts take spoliation seriously, but you have to act before the window closes.

If you're trying to figure out next steps after a Dallas truck accident, the priority is getting legal representation quickly. A site like wreckmatch.com can match you with vetted truck accident attorneys in Texas who handle evidence preservation as a first move — not an afterthought. Don't wait on this one.
